noTransferTaxNote: New Zealand has no stamp duty or transfer tax. This is the estimated cost of other buying expenses.

legal.label: Legal/conveyancing fees
legal.note: Costs vary based on complexity of the transaction and solicitor rates.

buildingInspection.label: Building inspection
buildingInspection.note: Recommended for all purchases. May be higher for larger or older properties.

valuation.label: Valuation (registered valuer)
valuation.note: Required by most lenders. Cost varies by property type and location.

lim.label: LIM report (Land Information Memorandum)
lim.note: Issued by the local council. Price set by the council — varies by district.

firstHomeLoan.label: Low equity premium (First Home Loan)
firstHomeLoan.note: First Home Loan backed by Kāinga Ora. A one-off premium of approximately 1.2% of the loan amount is charged by the lender.

standard.label: Low equity premium (standard)
standard.note: When deposit is less than 20%, most lenders charge a low equity premium (LEP) or margin of approximately 0.5%–2% of the loan amount as a one-off fee or rate add-on. Exact amount varies by lender.

gst.newBuildNote: 15% GST may be included in the purchase price of new builds. Check with your solicitor whether the price is GST-inclusive or GST-exclusive.

brightLineTest.investorNote: If you sell this property within 2 years, any profit may be taxed as income under the bright-line test.

interestDeductibility.investorNote: From April 2025, 100% of mortgage interest is deductible against rental income for residential investment properties.
